estimate.narrative_context: Probate cost avoided on sizeable estate (1-3% of probate estate); foundational, not estate-tax saving on its own.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Foundational estate-plan vehicle that avoids probate (1-3% of probate estate), provides incapacity protection, and serves as architecture for irrevocable trusts.

scenario_range.computation_method: 1-3% of estimated probate estate of ~$5M (excluding business interest, post-recap structure)

Execute powers of attorney, healthcare directives, HIPAA, and guardianship designations (see REC-EST-002).

sub_steps: Record deeds for East Cobb residence and Blue Ridge home

estimate.narrative_context: Direct attorney-fee cost for full document set; avoidance value far higher (probate, conservatorship, family disputes).

action_items.description: Notarize and witness documents per Georgia state law requirements.
Distribute copies to clients, healthcare providers, successor trustees/agents, and PSA file.

recommendations._audit_notes: State A using rec file fee range. Olivia (19) needs adult HIPAA/POA per rec file note.

estimate.narrative_context: Federal estate tax avoided over 20-year program: $152K/yr × 20 yrs × 40% (excl. compound growth).

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Systematic $152K/year annual-exclusion gifting (4 donees × 2 spouses × $19K) removes ~$3M of principal plus compound growth from the taxable estate over 20 years.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: principal × 40%. High: principal + 7% compound growth × 40% per rec file worked example.

estimate.narrative_context: Federal estate tax avoided on $9M of ILIT-owned death benefit at 40%.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: ILIT-owned life insurance ($9M total face) passes outside the taxable estate, avoiding ~$3.6M of federal estate tax at 40%, with premiums funded via Crummey gifts within annual exclusion capacity.

scenario_range.computation_method: Face amount range $5M-$12M × 40% federal estate tax rate

action_items.description: Annual review of coverage adequacy and premium funding.

recommendations._audit_notes: State A; per rec file worked example: $9M ILIT-owned face × 40% = $3.6M estate tax avoided.

estimate.narrative_context: Cumulative estate-tax savings from receiving annual gifts, GRAT remainder, and IDGT distributions outside the estate (rec file figure for Holloway-scale).

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Per-child irrevocable trusts (one each for Olivia, James, Sophie) with HEMS-to-35 distribution standard serve as receiving vehicle for annual gifts, GRAT remainder, and IDGT sale — combined estate-tax savings $4M-$8M for Holloway-scale.

scenario_range.computation_method: Per rec file: combined estate-tax savings typically $4-8M for Holloway-scale clients

action_items.description: Estate attorney drafts three per-child irrevocable trust documents with HEMS-to-35 distribution standard and Crummey provisions.
Client selects trustees (corporate trustee preferred per firm default).
Trusts obtain EINs and open accounts.
Establish Crummey notice procedures and coordinate downstream GRAT/IDGT remainder structure.

recommendations._audit_notes: Defaulted to per-child structure per Holloway default in rec file; distribution age 35 from FR hard constraint.

estimate.narrative_context: Estimated value transferred to children's trusts free of gift tax over 3-year GRAT term using May 2026 §7520 of 5.0% and 18%/yr business growth assumption (vs ~22.7% trailing 3-yr CAGR).

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Three-year zeroed-out GRAT funded with $8.2M of post-discount non-voting holdco units; at the May 2026 §7520 hurdle of 5.0%, an 18%/year growth scenario transfers approximately $4.2M to the children's trusts free of gift tax with no use of lifetime exemption.

scenario_range.computation_method: Low: business stalls (annuity returns assets, no transfer). Mid: 18% growth per rec file. High: closer to 22.7% historical CAGR

Fund GRAT by transferring appraised non-voting interest; document §7520 rate at funding.
Coordinate annual annuity payments and FMV documentation across the 3-year term.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Sale of $25M of non-voting interest to an IDGT in exchange for a 9-year AFR installment note (~4.5% mid/long-term AFR, May 2026) transfers an estimated $8M-$15M of additional wealth outside the estate, augmented by grantor income-tax payments that effectively become gift-tax-free transfers.

scenario_range.computation_method: Per rec file Holloway example: $8M-$15M+ depending on growth and transaction timing

Annual administration: note interest payments, grantor-trust 1041 statement, valuation tracking, note repayment per schedule.

estimate.narrative_context: Federal estate tax avoided over 25-year horizon: $5M funded × 7%/yr growth × 25 yrs → $27M outside estate × 40%.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: SLAT funded with $5M of Marcus's $15M lifetime exemption; over 25 years at 7% growth, ~$27M passes outside both estates, avoiding ~$10.8M of federal estate tax while preserving Catherine's lifetime access.

scenario_range.computation_method: Funding $5M × (1.07)^25 × 40% midpoint; low/high reflect 5%/9% growth scenarios

action_items.description: Estate attorney drafts SLAT with HEMS distribution standard, independent trustee, and structuring to avoid §2036 inclusion and reciprocal trust doctrine.

Trust EIN, bank account, and ongoing administration with annual trustee reports.

recommendations._audit_notes: State A using rec file worked example. Cautioned status preserved per rec file metadata; reciprocal trust doctrine and divorce risks flagged in client framing.

estimate.narrative_context: GST tax avoided on $30M of GST-exempt funding growing at 5%/yr over 60 years (rec file worked example): $30M×(1.05)^60 ≈ $560M; 40% GST rate.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Allocating the couple's $30M GST exemption to long-term dynasty trusts shelters multi-generational wealth — at 5%/year over 60 years, avoids ~$224M of GST tax that would otherwise apply at child-to-grandchild transfer.

scenario_range.computation_method: Sensitivity to growth rate (3-7%) and horizon (40-80 yrs); rec file midpoint at 5% / 60 yrs

action_items.description: Track GST exemption allocation and inclusion-ratio across all funding events.

recommendations._audit_notes: State A per rec file worked example. Plan section assigned to Estate Planning; could also surface in Specialty if multi-state situs chosen.

estimate.narrative_context: Computed at the Blue Ridge vacation home ($1.4M, no mortgage), 12-year term, May 2026 §7520 of 5.00%.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: 12-year QPRT on the Blue Ridge vacation home at the May 2026 §7520 of 5.0% would gift ~$350K of exemption against a residence projected to grow toward $2.1M-$2.5M, avoiding roughly $700K of federal estate tax — borderline given Catherine's potential real-estate development intent.

scenario_range.computation_method: Sensitivity to growth and term-end value; rec file pattern with Blue Ridge $1.4M residence (Holloway plan)

sub_steps: Donor pays property tax/insurance during term

recommendations._audit_notes: State A computed using illustrative §7520 mortality factor (rec file pattern; precise factor requires actuarial tables). Decision-needed flag set: borderline trigger, depends on Catherine's RE development intent.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Beneficiary designation audit prevents probate, family disputes, and stretch-IRA loss on retirement, life-insurance, and TOD/POD accounts — savings highly variable but routinely catastrophic when omitted.

action_items.description: Inventory all accounts with beneficiary designations (401(k), IRAs, life insurance, annuities, TOD/POD, HSA).
Compare current designations against new estate plan and family situation; identify updates needed.
Submit updated designations via custodian forms and retain confirmation receipts in central log.
Annual review of designations.
